title: Bridge Essay: Ayahuasca Therapy: Possible Reduction of Suicidal Ideation in Treatment-Resistant Depression - A Systematic Review.
concept: Nonordinary states as controlled experiments in consciousness — predictive processing breakdown and revision.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary states as controlled experiments in consciousness — predictive processing breakdown and revision.

**Ayahuasca Therapy: Possible Reduction of Suicidal Ideation in Treatment-Resistant Depression - A Systematic Review.** - Journal of psychoactive drugs (2026). DOI: 10.1080/02791072.2026.2661580. PMID: 42023657. Suicide, particularly in treatment-resistant depression (TRD), remains a pressing global health issue, with over 700,000 annual deaths. Existing treatments often have limited efficacy and delayed onset, creating a need for rapid-acting interventions. Ayahuasca, a traditional Amazonian psychedelic, has shown potential for rapidly reducing suicidal ideation. Our systematic review evaluated the clinical evidence regarding ayahuasca's effects on suicidality. From 6,633 initial records, five studies
